source_exhibit: J-1 (State Addenda to Disclosure Document) and D-1 (State Addenda to Franchise Agreement)

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages clauses may be unenforceable under CA Civil Code §1671
overrides.why_it_matters: If unenforceable, franchisor cannot collect the 60-month royalty LD formula

overrides.override_summary: CA B&P §20000-20043 provides termination/non-renewal rights that may supersede FA
overrides.why_it_matters: State law may require additional notice and protections before termination

overrides.override_summary: NY choice of law provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA law may govern instead of NY for CA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: VA venue provision may not be enforceable under CA law
overrides.why_it_matters: CA franchisees may be able to litigate in CA

overrides.override_summary: General release does not waive rights under CA Franchise Investment Law or Franchise Relations Act
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot release franchise law claims on renewal or transfer

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve liability under Hawaii franchise laws
overrides.why_it_matters: Hawaii franchise law claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Termination/non-renewal affected by 815 ILCS 705/19 and 705/20
overrides.why_it_matters: Illinois law may provide additional termination protections

overrides.override_summary: Governing law/venue/jury waiver do not waive IL Franchise Disclosure Act rights
overrides.why_it_matters: IL franchise law applies regardless of NY choice of law

overrides.override_summary: MD law may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: MD franchise law protections apply

overrides.override_summary: May sue in MD for MD franchise law claims; 3-year SOL
overrides.why_it_matters: Venue for MD claims is in MD, not VA

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve liability under MD Franchise Registration and Disclosure Law
overrides.why_it_matters: Cannot release MD franchise law claims

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for good cause; 30 days cure required
overrides.why_it_matters: Non-curable defaults may be limited in MI

overrides.override_summary: Cannot prohibit franchisee from joining franchisee association
overrides.why_it_matters: Association rights protected

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require release that deprives franchisee of act protections (except post-FA settlements)
overrides.why_it_matters: Pre-closing releases limited

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require arbitration or litigation outside Michigan
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue provision void in MI

overrides.override_summary: Cannot refuse to renew without fair market value compensation (if term <5 years and noncompete applies)
overrides.why_it_matters: FMV compensation on non-renewal may apply in limited circumstances

overrides.override_summary: 90 days termination notice (60 days cure) and 180 days non-renewal notice required
overrides.why_it_matters: Significantly longer notice periods than FA standard (10-30 days)

overrides.override_summary: Liquidated damages prohibited (MN Rule 2860.4400J)
overrides.why_it_matters: 60-month royalty LD formula is void in MN

overrides.override_summary: Cannot require litigation outside MN or jury trial waiver
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchisees can litigate in MN with jury trial

overrides.override_summary: General release does not relieve liability under MN Franchise Investment Law
overrides.why_it_matters: MN franchise law claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Franchisee may terminate on any grounds available by law
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law provides additional termination grounds for franchisee

overrides.override_summary: NY Franchise Sales Act requires NY law to govern NY franchise law causes of action; NY GBL §680-695 may supersede inconsistent FA provisions
overrides.why_it_matters: NY franchise law applies regardless of FA provisions

overrides.override_summary: General release on renewal/transfer may not be enforceable under NY law
overrides.why_it_matters: Release requirements limited in NY

overrides.override_summary: ALL liquidated damages provisions deleted entirely
overrides.why_it_matters: No liquidated damages whatsoever in ND

overrides.override_summary: ND law governs (supersedes NY choice of law)
overrides.why_it_matters: NY law does not apply to ND franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue provision deleted; jury trial waiver deleted
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchisees litigate in ND with jury trial

overrides.override_summary: Release does not relieve ND Franchise Disclosure Act liability
overrides.why_it_matters: ND franchise law claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Release on transfer/renewal does not apply to RI Franchise Investment Act claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchise law claims preserved

overrides.override_summary: Out-of-state venue/choice of law void for RI franchise law claims
overrides.why_it_matters: RI franchisees can litigate in RI under RI law

overrides.override_summary: Termination only for reasonable cause under VA Retail Franchising Act §13.1-564
overrides.why_it_matters: Some FA termination triggers may not constitute reasonable cause

overrides.override_summary: RCW 19.100.180 may supersede FA on termination and renewal
overrides.why_it_matters: WA law provides additional termination/renewal protections

overrides.override_summary: Release excludes WA Franchise Investment Protection Act rights (except negotiated settlements with independent counsel)
overrides.why_it_matters: WA franchise law claims preserved unless specifically negotiated

overrides.override_summary: Arbitration/mediation must be in WA or mutually agreed; litigation may be brought in WA
overrides.why_it_matters: VA venue provision overridden for WA franchisees

overrides.override_summary: Post-term noncompete void unless employee earns >$100K/year (adjusted for inflation); independent contractor >$250K. No-hire provisions between franchisees void.
overrides.why_it_matters: WA significantly limits noncompete enforcement (though HGI has no post-term noncompete anyway)

overrides.override_summary: Transfer fees limited to reasonable estimated or actual costs
overrides.why_it_matters: $200K COO fee may be challenged as exceeding actual costs
